expressions.english: to write
to write a letter
to write a novel
to write an e-mail
What did you write?
expressions.notes: -라고 is a verb ending used for citing or quoting a noun.

expressions.english: I have nothing to write.
expressions.notes: 말 = language; what one says; expression; word; term

expressions.english: When are you going to write it?
Please write your name.
How do you write your name?
Who wrote this?
When you finish writing, show it to me.
Do you want me to write it here?
expressions.notes:
여기 = here, this place
-(으)면 되다 = to be just supposed to + V; to just have to + V; can just + V

expressions.english: Please take a look at what I have written.
Write it all over again from the beginning.
expressions.notes:
처음 = first; for the first time
다시 = again

expressions.english: Write this in English.
I am in the middle of writing a report.
expressions.notes:
보고서 = report
중이다 = to be V-ing; to be between/middle/in the middle of + V-ing

expressions.english: To whom should I write this letter?
expressions.notes:
누구 = who
-이/이/여야 되다 = to have to + V

expressions.english: Did you read the e-mail I wrote to you?
expressions.notes: 봐다 = to see; to look; to watch; to meet up; to read

expressions.english: Have you written a letter recently?
expressions.notes:
최근에 = recently, lately
-(으)ㄴ 적이 있다 = to have done something

expressions.english: The book he wrote is interesting.
expressions.notes:
그 = that, the
사람 = person
책 = book
재미있다 = to be interesting
